review_title: I choose Tilda for creating clean, fast and creative web sites
description: I started with WordPress and used Tilda just for Landing pages. But as soon as I get to know Tilda better and notice that with a little open mind and lots of creativity you can do magic. I only needed their help a few times and they were fast and solve my problems. I really trust them with my work.
author: Tatjana B.
pros: It is simple to use and I love that they offer Zero blocks for more freedom in my designing. I also use their servers for Web sites that I create and never had any problems with any pages.
cons: I have nothing that I do not love. I am really in love with this platform and they are constantly adding new features and improving.

review_title: I love using Tilda
description: I think I did it already. I love every moment using Tilda. Thank you so much guys for creating such a wonderful and beautiful platform.
author: Akmal A.
pros: I love its intuitive, beautiful and minimalistic design. I love how the whole platform is set up and works. Sometimes it so happens that I make changes on my own website simply because I love exploring Tilda features and making them work on my website when published.
cons: I'm generally very satisfied with Tilda publishing. Perhaps, it would be great if you guys could improve Zero Block templates. For example, when I'm creating a new website using zero blocks I don't like to edit the same template for all other devices like mobile, tablet and so on. If I don't they look disorganzed and messy over there. I think it should be somehow synched so that no extra time is spent on editing them as well.

review_title: Clients from the UK can’t always access pages built on Tilda
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Wonderful capabilities for building landing pages. Ability to create beautiful design.
cons: Last time I used it some customers couldn’t open the site due to firewall because their servers were located in Russia.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A solid 9'8
description: Really good, if you use ZeroBlocks you are able to create whatever is in your heads. It's cheap and the support line is great
author: Miguel G.
pros: It's really easy to create a proffesional product for your business, that can compete with other webpages created coding.
cons: The Made on Tilda footer if you don't go for the year subscription. I think at least the business monthly plan should be able to remove it.

review_title: Flexible, easy to use, great support!
author: Elena E.
pros: I have been using Tilda Publishing for quite some time now for various projects. I find it very flexible and straight forward to use. It's interface is simple but at the same time offers strong features which I am happy to see they keep building and adding to it. It is quite stable and very importantly offers excellent support.
cons: Although it has different ways to handle multilingual sites, I find them not ideal for me and take quite some time to create and to update. I hope this will be something that will be handled. Also would be good to offer a file storage ability within Tilda.

review_title: Fantastic Website Builder and Hosting for a great price!
description: Overall it has been absolutely fantastic. I built my website in just 1 day and continue to grow it all of the time. I love that it is protected by Cloudflare, and how responsive the support team are. I have recently linked stripe payments and have started taking payments over the platform.
author: Chris B.
pros: Its ease of use - how quickly you can create beautiful websites! I have also had to contact the support team a few times for some more complex configurations and they have been wonderful!
cons: There are some features which require you to contact support. One example is the ability to restore your site from a backup. This is unlikely to be required by 99% of customers, so I acknowledge that my use case is unique. In saying this Tilda's support team have always been responsive and are able to resolve my problem on the first response.

review_title: Finally an application that hits the sweet spot between complexity and ease of use
description: A generous free trial, and quick and effective customer support means very little extraneous friction in the designing process.
author: Lars B.
pros: Ease of use is second to none. Streamlined yet flexible when I need it. I use it for class plans and I need to build hundreds of pages throughout a school year, and Tilda allows me to do this with very little friction. And the output is beautiful from very little effort.
cons: There is a 1GB cap for projects, but after having talked to support, they convinced me this is not an issue in real use, thanks to compression. Also, I use external content for videos etc. which is preferrable anyway.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Satisfied Tilda User
description: I love the platform. I have used it to build wesites for my clients and I am now using it for my own wesite.
author: Charmaine V.
pros: It's user friendly and if issues arise the support team sorts it out quickly. You don't need wesite building experience or coding to use Tilda.
cons: Nothing comes to mind. I think that Tilda is a good platform to use if you want to uild your website quickly.

review_title: Perfect Balance of Simplicity and Functionality
description: I've been using Tilda for a few months to build my business website and landing pages. The drag-and-drop interface, combined with beautiful pre-designed blocks, allowed me to create a professional website quickly. What impressed me most was the attention to detail - every template is modern and well-designed. The platform handles everything from hosting to SSL certificates, which saves me time and technical headaches. I've successfully launched multiple projects. The responsive design works flawlessly across all devices, and my clients always compliment the site's appearance and speed. While there's a learning curve for advanced features, the documentation and community support are excellent. Overall, Tilda strikes a perfect balance between ease of use and powerful functionality. It's ideal for entrepreneurs, small businesses, and creatives who want beautiful websites without hiring developers.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The visual block-based editor makes website creation incredibly intuitive - I can build professional-looking pages without coding knowledge. The extensive template library is outstanding, with hundreds of pre-designed blocks for every use case. I especially appreciate the fast loading speeds and built-in SEO optimization. The responsive design automatically adapts perfectly to mobile devices. The Zero Block feature gives me complete creative freedom when I need custom designs. Export functionality is excellent - I can download my site and host it anywhere. Customer support has been responsive and helpful whenever I had questions.
cons: The free plan is quite limited - you really need a paid subscription to unlock the full potential. Some advanced features like custom forms and CRM integration require the Business plan which can be pricey for small projects. The learning curve for Zero Block can be steep initially if you want pixel-perfect designs. Sometimes the interface can feel a bit slow when working with pages that have many blocks. I wish there were more integrations with third-party tools without needing Zapier.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: You get a lot for what you pay
description: Pretty good experience honestly. The learning curve was way gentler than I expected, and I had a functioning site within a few hours. Support has been helpful when I needed them.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Pretty affordable compared to other builders I've tried. You get a lot for what you pay, especially on the business plan. The free plan is actually usable, not like some platforms that barely let you do anything. Fair pricing overall.
cons: The templates are nice but very standardized. Hard to make your site stand out without spending hours in Zero Block customizing everything.

review_title: Give Tilda a try. You will not be disappointed!
description: It's been great! I was converted from Wordpress some five years ago after data crash, and honestly, I've never looked back!
author: Arjun J.
pros: It is very easy to go about, very intuitive, and the customer support is ace! Also, it is highly customisable.
cons: The pricing perhaps? I would rather it had been somewhat lesser. I do like it very much, but it does pinch the pocket.

review_title: My feedback on Tilda Publishing platform
description: Generally speaking, the platform is amazing, constantly improving, working with it is a pure pleasure.
author: Evelina U.
pros: I like the logic of the interface, wide design possibilities, ease of use and intuitiveness of Tilda. Wonderful knowledge base. 10/10 when when the client's needs match the platform's technical capabilities.
cons: I don't really like the fact that you can't upload video files. Editing options for AMP pages are limited, and Google console does not want to monitor them, it shows errors. It would be perfect if converting a standard block to a Zero would transfer the design of the standard block to it.

review_title: A scalable website builder for many purposes
author: Vera P.
pros: -Versatile (we built so many different pages on tilda, from promo pages to even landing pages to even our business site...) -Aesthetics -SEO: Tilda-made pages rank very well! -Easy to use: we use Tilda for our marketing tasks and everyone in the team can quickly modify something if necessary. It's like working with Google Docs, everyone knows how to edit something! -Teamwork available -Price is super affordable, templates are free
cons: Maybe more templates would be nice - they have many now but sometimes we have to redesign them a lot to make them relevant for our goal. For the rest, the platform is very nice!

review_title: DOES IT GET ANY BETTER? I DOUBT IT!
description: The final destination was well worth it. Having had a trial on the well known website platforms; Squarespace, Wix, Weebly and others, I decided to spend hours and hours, months and months seeking a website builder that suits our organisation and associated businesses. Tilda was in sight but I had to trial it first in order to see if it met all our expectations and it nearly met all. We did not wait a couple of weeks before our white label trial ended, it soon became apparent that Tilda was for us. We immediately signed up for the business annual plan. A decision we have not regretted. I am hoping that Tilda will consider allowing PDF and Word documents to be uploaded as this is an essential part of our organisation. Yes, we can use third party applications, but I would like simply to be able to have the facility to upload documents so visitors can download them at their will on the same platform as our website. The other important feature that we would like to see a membership secure area. Again, we have this via a third party but not ideal. If Tilda can provide this, we would be extremely happy and would be happy to pay a Tilda developer, subject to costs, to build this for us. Pricing is really good. But we do need to purchase another business plan and highly likely several business plans, but an overall business plan that will allow us unlimited websites would be very welcomed indeed. We can then move all our websites away from sitemaker.com for good, but the costs prevent us doing this at presentl Surely, Tilda is superb, far superior to the website builders we have seen and tested. Well worth the purchase and we look forward to years of using Tilda and being part of their future progress. Well done to Tilda. I have a strong feeling that over the next couple of years, we will hear a lot about Tilda and I am confident that they will become a market leader.
author: Libby E.
pros: Too many to mention
cons: PDF and Word documents are not allowed to be uploaded, so these are important to most organisations and businesses nowadays. A a secure member's area would be great as well as file uploads via the online form blocks.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Pretty good for beginners
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I came in a complete novice to web design, but I was able to get a great looking webpage built really quickly. As with anything the more intricate you wanna get on design, the more you have to learn, but it was pretty easy to figure out.
cons: The customer service and billing had been an issue at times. Long response time, and multiple tickets created for one question were really annoying.

review_title: The best website builder for non-designers and designers alike
description: Tilda enabled me to offer much higher quality websites for smaller projects and to prototype ideas much faster. It's especially good for early-stage projects, events, presentations and content research.
author: Pedro R.
pros: Tilda has probably the most intuitive interface and smallest learning curve of any software I've used. The quality of the templates is great from the start but they are also super customizable. And if you're a webdesigner you can do pretty much anything with it. I will probably never do another website from scratch for early designs or small initiatives.
cons: There are still a few small glitches to clean up and maybe need some work on the settings end.

review_title: My go-to for pages that look great and don't take too much time to create.
author: Igor G.
pros: You can't really mess up the design because Tilda won't let you, which means whatever you make will look decent. It has the most intuitive interface among all of the constructors I used. The free trial allows you to try all of the features, create and publish a website with everything you need and then keep it after a trial ends. You can export website and upload them manually to your server. You can create any type of pages with it and again, they will look good.
cons: Not the best in terms of SEO. So that needs some manual tweaking, like everywhere else. Still managed to rank all of the websites I created on Tilda.

review_title: Better Responsive Web Builder than Squarespace
description: This is by far the best web builder out there for the average Joe. Wix is great but to me always seemed a little tacky looking and slow. Wix also has terrible functioning mobile version. Tilda is the fastest, most professional and quickest web builder out there. Like others have said, it is a few features away from being the industry leader (Already is in my book). You can do more customization than a Squarespace while yet keeping the same clean look. You can still add HTML and CSS code to headers and everything site wide. People complain about the menu system of Tilda, which I agree at first was confusing. However, my needs were that of a website that had multiple menu systems throughout the site which certain web builders don't let you do. Tilda was perfect for this and honestly met almost every requirement I needed for a web builder. Did I mention the responsiveness of this site is incredible.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: -Zero Block editing like wix but is responsive. -FAST SITE -Multiple Menu Options -Clean Layout -Tons of features
cons: -Still needs document hosting so I not have to use dropbox or drive. -Customer service doesn't respond until midnight or later because they are based in Russia. -No great option for a video gallery
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Perfect balance between easy of use and functionality.
description: I managed to create by myself an amazing looking landing page for my start-up.
author: Tiago B.
pros: Easy of use for users without technical knowledge nor experience building website. Great offer in terms of blocks and amazing design. Simple placement of pictures.
cons: Integration with Mailchimp could be better. GDPR compliance should be more visible in the available forms.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: The best way to test your concept
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Tilda helps us to test ideas and concepts, to assemble web-pages promptly. Right now we make Pitch Decks and presentations on Tilda.
cons: Easy to use, low price and great support

review_title: Great interface
description: Tilda helped me save over thousand euros I made my website myself it is really easy to use and their blocks and typography features are really beautiful and stylish.
author: Anatol G.
pros: Design, usability, price, support team
cons: None.

review_title: Powerful no-code solution
description: I’m a web designer and I’ve been working with Tilda since 2020. During this time, I’ve created various types of websites for my clients and used the platform for projects of different complexity. Overall, my experience has been very positive. Tilda is reliable, flexible, and well-suited for both simple and more advanced tasks
author: Ekaterina S.
pros: I love that Tilda lets you build websites of any complexity without knowing how to code. The platform is constantly improving, and the new features make the workflow faster and easier. Their support team is also great. They always reply within the stated time and help resolve any issues. And the websites built on Tilda perform well in terms of SEO
cons: Sometimes creating responsive layouts takes longer than I’d like, because many elements need to be adjusted manually. Unlike platforms with automatic grid behavior, Tilda requires more hands-on work to adapt blocks for different screen sizes

review_title: Great visual builder with slow support
description: I’m still in the process of building my website on Tilda, so my experience is limited. So far, the platform gives a good impression in terms of design capabilities and layout flexibility. However, the lack of fast customer support and more detailed instructions makes it harder to move forward quickly. A quicker way to reach out to the support team would make a big difference.
author: Kateryna B.
pros: Tilda Publishing offers a beautiful, modern visual editor and a variety of ready-made templates that let you create a website without coding knowledge. The interface is intuitive, especially for users who want to build and customize their site on their own. It's great that you can upload custom fonts, manage responsiveness, and optimize for SEO. Integration with external services is also well thought out, allowing smooth connection with tools like payment systems, email marketing platforms, or analytics.
cons: The support team takes a long time to respond, and there is no option to call them like on some other platforms. Sometimes it’s hard to figure things out, and the documentation doesn't always provide answers to my specific questions. This can slow down the process for beginners.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great service, highly recommended
description: Customer service is very fast, professional and problem solving oriented. Overall we are very satisfied with Tilda and will continue to make websites for our customers that are very satisfied with our work
author: Raivis Z.
pros: Tilda makes web design simple and stylish. With ready made blocks and Zero for custom design, strong SEO tools, and smooth responsiveness, it’s perfect for building fast, modern sites without coding hassle.
cons: Restrictions on page count and storage space can be a drawback, websites with extensive content or large media libraries may quickly hit those caps.

review_title: Great Service - Simple, Professional, Just Works.
description: Very good - the service works, it is easy to use, and support has been amazing when needed (which has been rare). Overall very pleased with Tilda and recommend clean alternative to Wordpress or Wix.
author: David B.
pros: Simplicity - nice professional and clean layout templates - the ability to build your own blocks quickly for custom layouts when needed. Support has been amazing on the rare occasions I have needed it. As a small business with a few products Tilda is a great fit for managing a few websites with ease.
cons: Some pre-build template sections are very close to what I am looking for - but missing some controls that you wish were there. So you end up building your own block (which takes time). The CRM functionality is not really "CRM" - small business need more than what they provide - best to not call it CRM ... more like a "list capture" of contacts that can be moved around to other systems.
